name: the primitive type called ian

phase_0.boundary_condition: The Relational Mode interface for lateral peer-to-peer exchange.

VINIT.justification: The state of a potential peer before any lateral connection is established.

TANCH.justification: The formal definition of the lateral relational mode that contains all peer exchanges.

AFWD.justification: A directed transmission from one peer toward another.

AREV.justification: The return transmission that completes the lateral handshake.

CLINK.justification: The sequential chaining of request and response into a stable connection.

IMSCRIB.justification: The self-recognition of a node as a valid participant in the ian mode.

FSPLIT.justification: The bifurcation of a single connection into simultaneous transmit and receive paths.

FFUSE.justification: The reconstitution of transmit and receive paths into a unified lateral relation.

EVALT.justification: The affirmative state where both peers share a consistent data view.

EVALF.justification: The failure state where peer data views diverge.

ENGAGR.justification: A state where data is both sent and not yet acknowledged, held in transit.

IFIX.justification: The permanent append-only record of the lateral exchange.

steps.domain_action: initiate discovery probe to lateral neighbor
unify paths into a stable symmetric link
bind link to the active session
hold unverified packets in the asynchronous buffer
record the successful handshake in the transaction log
anchor the session within the ian relational boundary

phase_6.cycle_cost: network overhead and local cache updates
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The symmetry of the ian mode ensures that information exchange is balanced and reversible.
